# cited-research (Claude Code skill)

A Claude Code skill that produces citation-backed research documents with
independent verification. Every claim traces to a web source visited during
the session, and isolated sub-agents audit the output before it is handed
back to the user.

**Core principle:** nothing is true until a source says it is, and a
separate reviewer confirms the source actually says it.

The methodology separates research, writing, and verification into distinct
phases, and makes the writing phase aware that verification follows. This
makes hallucination structurally difficult rather than relying on the model
to self-police.

## What this repo contains

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| [`SKILL.md`](SKILL.md) | The skill instructions Claude Code loads at invocation. Phase 0 plan mode through Phase 5 index maintenance. |
| [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) | Contributor guidance for editing this skill. |
| [`references/`](references/) | Sub-agent prompt templates, citation format, update workflow, and the research-basis evidence log. |
| [`scripts/multi_search.py`](scripts/multi_search.py) | Multi-engine search helper the coordinator runs between discovery iterations to reduce single-engine bias. |
| [`tests/`](tests/) | Unit tests for `multi_search.py`. Run with `make check`. |
| [`TEST_PLAN.md`](TEST_PLAN.md) | Test rationale and scope. |

## Installing the skill

Claude Code loads skills from `~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`.
To install:

```bash
# Clone somewhere stable
git clone https://github.com/jewzaam/claude-skill-cited-research.git

# Symlink (or copy) into the Claude Code skills directory
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills
ln -s "$(pwd)/claude-skill-cited-research" ~/.claude/skills/cited-research

# Build the skill's private venv so the multi-engine search helper works
cd ~/.claude/skills/cited-research
make install-dev
```

The `make install-dev` step creates `.venv/` inside the skill directory
and installs `ddgs` plus dev dependencies. The skill invokes the helper
by the absolute path `~/.claude/skills/cited-research/.venv/bin/python
~/.claude/skills/cited-research/scripts/multi_search.py`, so it runs
correctly regardless of your working directory when Claude Code uses it.

If you skip `make install-dev`, the skill still works, but sub-agents
fall back to Claude's built-in WebSearch only — you lose the
multi-engine diversity boost documented in
[`references/research-basis.md`](references/research-basis.md)
§Multi-Engine Search Diversity.

Restart Claude Code (or start a new session). The skill triggers
automatically on research-style prompts — "compare X and Y", "what does
the research say", "cite your sources" — or you can invoke it explicitly.

## Using the skill

Start any research request in a new Claude Code session. The skill enters
plan mode, proposes research dimensions, surfaces framing assumptions,
and asks how to handle counter-perspectives before dispatching any
sub-agents. Output is written to `research/<topic-slug>/` inside whatever
repo you are in, with:

- `README.md` — standalone summary and decision framework
- `<deliverable>.md` — full analysis with inline citations
- `citations.md` — numbered source list with extracted data
- `references/<dimension>.md` — per-dimension detail files
- `audit/` — citation-audit and consistency-review outputs

When the repo name is literally `cited-research`, the skill also maintains
a root-level `index.md` of all topics. Otherwise the topic directory is
self-contained.

## Developing the script helper

The `scripts/multi_search.py` helper has a standard Python project
layout. Run the full validation suite with:

```bash
make check       # format, lint, typecheck, test, coverage
make test-live   # optional: hits real DuckDuckGo
make help        # list all targets
```

The Makefile owns venv creation under `.venv/`.

## Design evidence

Every non-obvious design decision in `SKILL.md` has a backing entry in
[`references/research-basis.md`](references/research-basis.md) with
citations. When changing a behavior documented there, update the evidence
alongside the code.
